title: Executive Order 9087—Transfer of Jurisdiction Over Certain Lands Containing Oil and Gas Deposits From the War Department to the Department of the Interior

flags.justification: A narrow, technical transfer of a specific subset of jurisdiction (oil and gas deposits only) between two federal agencies.
Sec. 4 requires all royalties to be paid into the Treasury and credited to miscellaneous receipts — a specific financial-accountability provision.
Single purpose despite covering many individual land parcels.
A jurisdictional transfer creating no compliance regime against private parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A permanent jurisdictional transfer, not a temporary-authority grant.
Sec. 3 explicitly subordinates Interior's new jurisdiction to the War Department's continuing primary jurisdiction for flood-control and navigation purposes — a careful, protective priority clause.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A narrow, resource-protective action preventing revenue loss to the government through drainage, a routine application of established transfer authority.
The explicit subordination clause preserves the War Department's primary mission while enabling Interior's mineral-management role — well-layered, non-conflicting jurisdiction.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. An extremely lengthy order (extensive metes-and-bounds legal descriptions) but structurally simple and well-designed.
